Building
products
that matter.
hero.supportingCopy: We design and build hardware, software, and physical products that work together to solve real problems.

projects.heading: Our Products
projects.subheadline: Products that combine hardware, software, and physical design to solve real problems.

justin.name: JustIn
justin.description: Wireless scoring system for fencing. Every touch, counted. Built by a fencer, for fencers — live scoring and training analytics in your pocket.

zutto.name: Zutto
zutto.description: A mobile platform that turns everyday habits into engaging experiences through gamification and progress tracking.

about.heading: Who We Are
about.studioBody: Nytsu is a product studio dedicated to thoughtful design, practical functionality, and seeing great ideas shipped. Every product we make carries our mark: built with purpose, owned end-to-end, and refined until it’s right.

hero.body: Built by a fencer, for fencers — live scoring and training analytics in your pocket.

waitlist.heading: Be first to fence with it.
waitlist.subheading: JustIn is in development. Add your email on our short form and we'll reach out when it's ready.
waitlist.ctaAriaLabel: Join the JustIn waitlist — opens Google Form in a new tab

foundation.heading:
Scoring made
for athletes.
foundation.subheading: JustIn brings wireless scoring and real-time analytics to every fencing practice — no scoring machines, no cables, no hassle.

cards.label: What it does
cards.title: Live scoring in your pocket.
cards.body: JustIn tracks every touch wirelessly, records your bouts, and gives you real-time stats. Open the app, tap Start Bout, and you're scoring in under a minute.

cards.label: Training tool
cards.title: Built for practice, not paperwork.
cards.body: JustIn is a training and analytics platform, not FIE competition hardware. That means no regulatory friction — it works for every club, coach, and athlete worldwide.

cards.label: Who it's for
cards.title: Fencers 14+ and their coaches.
cards.body: From competitive juniors to serious recreational adults. English and Spanish supported. Designed for athletes who want real data without professional-grade complexity.

cards.label: Made by Nytsu
cards.title: Designed by Nytsu.
cards.body: JustIn is a Nytsu product — designed, built, and refined end-to-end by the same team that competes with it. Every detail comes from real training sessions, not a spec sheet.

screens.heading:
Five screens.
One flow.
screens.subheading: Home → Setup → Live Bout → Result → Stats. The bout screen has no navigation — it is the navigation.

scoring.heading:
The rules,
in plain terms.
scoring.subheading: JustIn follows standard fencing scoring conventions. These guidelines define how the app counts, stores, and displays every touch.

rules.title: One touch, one point
rules.body: Each valid touch scores exactly one point to the left or right fencer. Points are logged the moment the scoring button is pressed — there is no delayed confirmation.

rules.title: 3 periods × 3 minutes
rules.body: A standard bout runs three periods of three minutes each. Scoring resets between periods. The total score accumulates across all three periods.

rules.title: First to 15 wins
rules.body: The first fencer to reach 15 touches wins the bout, regardless of the period. If neither reaches 15, the highest score at the end of period 3 decides the winner.

rules.title: Undo is always available
rules.body: Any point can be removed with a single Undo tap immediately after it is scored. This accounts for referee corrections, hardware misfires, or human error.

rules.title: Simultaneous touches
rules.body: In épée, simultaneous valid touches award one point to each fencer. The app supports double-touch logging — both scores increment at the same time.

rules.title: Scores are local-first
rules.body: All scores are recorded on-device the moment they happen. Cloud sync happens in the background. A lost connection never interrupts a live bout.

principles.heading:
As fast as setting
up a piste.
principles.subheading: A standard piste takes 2–3 minutes. That is the maximum time from opening the app to scoring the first touch.

rules.title: One tap to the most important thing
rules.body: From anywhere, the critical action is never more than one tap away. Start Bout from Home. Undo during Bout. Next Bout from Result.

rules.title: Score numbers are always legible
rules.body: Never smaller than 60px during a bout. Readable from 1 metre in gym lighting. Color alone distinguishes left from right.

rules.title: Nothing irreversible without warning
rules.body: End Bout requires a 1.5s hold. Delete requires confirmation. Undo is always one tap. The athlete never loses data by accident.

rules.title: The bout screen has no distractions
rules.body: During scoring, navigation disappears. Notifications suppressed. Only: score, timer, two scoring buttons.

rules.title: Defaults are always correct
rules.body: 3 periods × 3 minutes. An athlete who changes nothing still gets a valid, properly configured bout.

rules.title: Works offline, syncs when ready
rules.body: Scores and records locally. Syncs when connectivity returns. Bad wifi doesn't break the app.
